flags.note: Definition d="You read it on a tablet or mobile phone." states a property, not an identity, so mc distractors "calendar" and "timetable" satisfy it literally (you can read either on a tablet) and the carrier "Open the school ___ and read it on your tablet." admits them too. They are different objects, not true synonyms of "webpage", so per the mc calibration this is warn not fix; it is the known accepted property-style-definition warn. Tighten the definition to a webpage-specific cue to retire it.
